metadata.reliability_note: Map produced from pasted English text (NLV-style rendering). Hebrew was not consulted. For STA Studio testing only.

classification.local_register_shifts: v.8 closes with an INTIMATE shift inside Jonah's quoted complaint. The narrator's voice across vv.5-8 is steady and observational; the divine actions are reported plainly without commentary.
classification.suggested_pace: deliberate, with each "appointment" — plant, worm, wind — landing as a separate beat

level_1.prose_arc: The passage moves from Jonah positioning himself to watch the city's fate, through three divine appointments — plant, worm, wind — that swing him from comfort to misery and finally to a spoken wish to die. The arc traces withdrawal, shelter, brief joy, sudden loss, exposure, and despair. The burden is Jonah's unresolved anger over God's mercy on Nineveh, displaced now onto a single plant that briefly makes him happy. The concern is whether Jonah will see what God is doing through these small living and natural things, or only feel the heat on his head.
level_1.context: The setting is just outside Nineveh, immediately after Jonah's message has been delivered and the city has repented. Jonah leaves the city and chooses a position east of it, where the morning sun rises, and builds a small shelter to wait. The reader needs to know that Jonah is still angry at God's mercy from the previous scene, and that the LORD God is here staging a small private parable for one prophet, using natural elements — plant, worm, hot east wind, sun — each "appointed" by God in turn. The passage assumes a hearer who knows the earlier chapters of Jonah.
level_1.emotion_tone: The tone moves through sharp swings — from watchful distance, to brief delight, to bodily misery and a spoken wish to die. The emotional weight sits in Jonah's exposed unguardedness rather than in any rhetorical heightening; his joy is vivid and short, his suffering concrete and felt on the head. A quieter ironic weight runs underneath, carried by the calm threefold pattern of divine sending — plant, worm, wind — each landing softly while producing an outsized effect on the prophet's body and heart.
level_1.communicative_function: The passage stages a teaching encounter between God and his prophet through objects and weather rather than direct rebuke. It records the prophet's continuing resistance and prepares the ground for the closing question of the book of Jonah. Speaking the passage rehearses how God can use small things to unsettle a hardened messenger, and how a prophet's own discomfort can become the place where mercy is being shown.

scenes.title: Withdrawal and watching post

people.name: Jonah
people.role: prophet who withdraws to watch the city
people.relationship: prophet of the LORD, the recently obeyed-but-resentful messenger sent to Nineveh
people.wants: to see what becomes of the city
people.carries: continuing anger at God's mercy, prophetic identity, watchful vigil

people.name: The Lord God (referenced, not present)
people.role: the sender Jonah continues to resist
people.relationship: covenant God whose mercy on Nineveh has unsettled Jonah
people.carries: divine authority that frames Jonah's withdrawal

places.name: The city (Nineveh)
places.type: foreign capital recently warned and repentant
places.effect_on_scene: defines what Jonah is watching for

places.name: East of the city
places.effect_on_scene: places Jonah in the path of the morning sun

places.name: Tent / booth
places.type: improvised covering of branches or cloth
places.meaning: temporary shade for a settled wait

objects.name: Shadow of the tent
objects.signals: that protection from heat is already a concern

objects.name: Watching / waiting
objects.signals: an unresolved expectation about the city's fate

scenes.what_happens: Jonah leaves the city, sits to its east, builds a tent, and waits in its shade to see what will happen to the city.

scenes.title: The plant and Jonah's joy

people.name: The Lord God
people.relationship: covenant God acting toward his prophet
people.wants: to teach Jonah by means of the plant

people.name: Jonah
people.wants: relief from heat and from his suffering
people.carries: prior anger now temporarily eased by shade

places.name: The watching post (continuing from Scene 1)
places.type: open ground east of the city
places.meaning: vantage point now improved by shade
places.effect_on_scene: sets the contrast between earlier shelter and the new plant

objects.name: Plant
objects.what_it_is: a fast-growing plant raised up by God
objects.signals: God's direct action through a small living thing

objects.name: Jonah's suffering
objects.what_it_is: heat-driven distress, both bodily and inner
objects.signals: that comfort, not approval, is what is being given

objects.name: Joy
objects.what_it_is: Jonah's strong gladness about the plant
objects.signals: that Jonah's mood is bound to a single small object

scenes.what_happens: The Lord God causes a plant to grow up over Jonah to shade him from the hot sun and to ease his suffering, and Jonah is very happy about the plant.
scenes.communicative_purpose: Shows divine provision producing brief, sharp joy.

scenes.title: The worm and the plant's destruction

people.name: God
people.relationship: the same God who sent the plant
people.wants: to take back what was given
people.carries: divine authority over creatures and plants

people.name: Jonah (referenced, present in setting but not acting)
people.role: still seated under what is being destroyed
people.relationship: prophet whose comfort is being removed
people.carries: an attachment to the plant that is about to be tested

places.name: The space over Jonah
places.type: same spot above Jonah where the plant grew
places.effect_on_scene: marks the reversal of the previous scene

objects.name: Worm
objects.what_it_is: a small creature appointed by God
objects.signals: God's reversal carried out through a small living thing

objects.name: Plant
objects.signals: that what God gives, God may also remove

objects.name: Time-marker — beginning of the next day
objects.what_it_is: the moment of the worm's action

objects.name: Drying up
objects.what_it_is: the plant's withering after the worm's work

scenes.what_happens: At the beginning of the next day, God sends a worm that destroys the plant, and the plant dries up.
scenes.communicative_purpose: Reverses the previous comfort by divine appointment.

scenes.title: Wind, sun, and the death wish

people.name: God
people.role: sender of the hot east wind
people.wants: to bring Jonah to the breaking point
people.carries: divine authority over wind and weather

places.name: The watching post (now without shade)
places.type: open ground east of the city, formerly under the plant
places.meaning: place where the morning sun strikes without barrier
places.effect_on_scene: lets the heat fall directly on Jonah

places.name: The east (direction)
places.meaning: line along which heat advances toward Jonah
places.effect_on_scene: shapes the wind that adds to the sun's heat

objects.name: Hot east wind
objects.what_it_is: a scorching wind appointed by God
objects.function_in_scene: third divine sending after plant and worm
objects.signals: God's continuing action through natural elements

objects.name: Sun
objects.function_in_scene: source of the heat striking him

objects.name: Jonah's head
objects.what_it_is: body part on which the heat falls

objects.name: Weakness
objects.what_it_is: faintness brought on by the heat
objects.signals: that the body has reached its limit

objects.name: Spoken complaint
objects.what_it_is: Jonah's spoken comparison of death and life
objects.function_in_scene: speech turning point of the passage

objects.name: Reply from God (absent)
objects.what_it_is: any divine response within this passage
objects.signals: a held silence that the next verses will break

scenes.what_happens: When the sun rises, God sends a hot east wind; the sun beats on Jonah's head until he becomes weak, and he begs with all his heart to die, saying that death is better to him than life.
scenes.communicative_purpose: Pushes Jonah to a spoken death-wish.
scenes.significant_absence: God does not yet answer Jonah within this passage.

concept_bank.note: The Hebrew alternates among YHWH Elohim, ha-Elohim, and Elohim across vv.6-8. The English flattens these, but the alternation may matter for STA testing and for languages that use different divine names. Needs controlled handling.
A specific fast-growing plant; some traditions identify it as castor-oil plant or gourd. The English here says only "plant." Needs careful target rendering.
A small creature, but here divinely "appointed" using the same verb pattern used earlier in Jonah for the great fish. The pattern itself carries weight beyond the creature.
A specific Mediterranean weather phenomenon (sirocco-type); not just "warm air." Needs target-language equivalent that signals scorching, dry, unbearable heat.
Directional language with possible covenantal echoes (Eden, exile, judgment); test map flags for review.
A recurring Hebrew Bible pattern (compare Elijah, Job). May warrant controlled handling so the despair register is not flattened into casual complaint.

figure_flags.intended_meaning: a temporary shaded waiting place, not a dwelling
figure_flags.review_note: Target cultures with no booth tradition may need help understanding it as a temporary shade-structure, not a tent for travel.

figure_flags.surface_image: God dispatching small creatures and natural forces
figure_flags.intended_meaning: sovereign placement of small things to act on a person
figure_flags.review_note: This pattern is structural across the book of Jonah and should not be reduced to ordinary "happened" language.
Target language may have its own whole-person idiom (whole liver, whole soul, whole insides).

figure_flags.figure: Death is better to me than life
figure_flags.review_note: Keep the comparative form if the target language allows; otherwise render as a strong wish to die.

figure_flags.intended_meaning: weather as an instrument of suffering, divinely sent
figure_flags.review_note: Needs a brief cultural footing if the target audience does not know desert wind effects.
